title: Make the DQ gate outcome durably observable — gold_quality_history cannot answer 'did the gate pass on run X'
description: task-026 wired the data-quality gate and it works, but its outcome is not recoverable after the fact. gold_quality_history persists refresh_timestamp, layer, entity, metric_name, metric_value, threshold, breach_flag — and NOT the per-check `status` field that actually drives the gate. The halt is decided by result['status'] == 'fail' over the BLOCKING_CHECKS set (data_quality_checks:1817); breach_flag is an unrelated score threshold, score < 70.0 (:1646). The two diverge in practice: grain_uniqueness failing on 2 bad grains out of 2561 rows scores ~99.9 and does NOT breach, while still halting the pipeline. Once a run's notebook output scrolls away (or the editor session is reloaded, which empties the Output panel), the only durable record cannot distinguish a clean run from a blocked one. Two secondary defects in the same table: silver-to-gold2:2305 also appends to gold_quality_history (10 rows/run) alongside data_quality_checks:1679 (52 rows/run) with no producer discriminator, and silver-to-gold2:2669 consequently reports twice the true pipeline-run count by counting DISTINCT refresh_timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: gold_quality_history persists the per-check `status` produced by log_check_result, so a past run's gate outcome is reconstructable from the table alone. The table is append-only and already holds history, so the schema change must be explicit (ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMNS, or a deliberate mergeSchema on write) and existing rows must stay readable — pre-change rows carrying NULL status is acceptable and expected.

A per-run gate verdict is durably recorded, not left to be re-derived by intersecting persisted rows against the BLOCKING_CHECKS set in notebook source: at minimum the blocking-failure count and whether the aggregated raise fired. 'Did the gate pass on run X' must be answerable by a single query with no reference to notebook code.

Rows written by silver-to-gold2:2305 are distinguishable from those written by data_quality_checks:1679 (a producer/source column, or an equivalent already-unambiguous discriminator). Today both writers append to the same table with no marker.
silver-to-gold2:2669's printed pipeline-run count reflects real pipeline runs rather than DISTINCT refresh_timestamp, which currently double-counts because each run produces one timestamp per writer.

The breach_flag/status distinction is documented where the DQ framework is described (.claude/support/documents/data_quality_framework.md or data_quality_architecture.md): breach_flag is a score threshold (score < 70) and is NOT the gate; the gate is status == 'fail' over BLOCKING_CHECKS. Include the concrete counter-example so the trap is legible.

[Erik/Fabric] After deploy + run, gold_quality_history alone answers the gate question for a clean run AND for a deliberately-failed run (inject a blocking failure, e.g. duplicate procurement transactions). Historical rows predating this change are NOT backfilled — the criterion applies to runs from this change forward.
notes: CREATED 2026-07-22, surfaced while measuring task-026 crit 6 on the first fully green pipeline run. The acceptance pack asserted 'DQ gate passed' from breach_flag and got a PASS that was not evidence of anything — the check was reading the wrong field. The gate had in fact passed, but only the pipeline activity's own success proved it.

PROOF OF THE DIVERGENCE, from live data rather than inference: gold_quality_history's 18:29 and 18:35 UTC runs both record 0 breaches, yet task-026's notes record that same run (20:16 local) as having 1 blocking failure — grain_uniqueness@fact_supply_share. A check can fail the gate and never breach, because 2 duplicate grains out of 2561 rows scores ~99.9 against a 70.0 threshold.

WHY THE OUTPUT PANEL IS NOT A SUBSTITUTE: per project memory (driving-fabric-from-claude-code), the pipeline editor's Output panel only shows runs from the CURRENT editor session — on a fresh page load it is empty, and that emptiness is not evidence of no runs. So the notebook's printed BLOCKING-CHECK ENFORCEMENT block is not a durable record either.

TIMEZONE GOTCHA for anyone querying this table: refresh_timestamp is written in UTC while the Fabric UI and every prior task note use local time (UTC+2 CEST). A correct, current measurement reads two hours 'early' and looks stale. Decoded run history is recorded in task-026's notes.

SCOPE BOUNDARY: this task does NOT change which checks are blocking, the severity model, or the aggregated-raise design — all of that is task-026's and works. This is purely about making the outcome durable and the table's two writers legible.

FILE COORDINATION: touches data_quality_checks (task-026's file) and silver-to-gold2 (shared with task-038 and the alias/commodity seed work). Do not run in parallel with tasks editing those files; dependency on task-026 is recorded to force ordering.

IMPLEMENTATION HINT (not a decision — the implementer should confirm): the two writers already differ in row count and metric-name shape (data_quality_checks writes metric_name='dq_{check_name}' plus dq_dimension_*/dq_overall_*), so a discriminator may be derivable without a schema change; but an explicit column is clearer and is what criterion 3 asks for. Note that silver-to-gold2's writer has no notion of blocking checks at all, so status will legitimately be NULL or 'n/a' for its rows — which itself discriminates the producers.

IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-22 (implement-agent). gold_quality_history widened 7 -> 9 columns (status, producer). silver-to-gold2 owns the DDL and now declares the 9-column shape; a new idempotent ensure_quality_history_columns() runs explicit ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMNS against any pre-existing table — no mergeSchema, no recreate, no backfill. ADD COLUMNS appends at the end so both writers emit columns in exact table order. data_quality_checks now persists result['status'] per check (previously computed, printed, dropped) and stamps producer='data_quality_checks'; aggregate rows carry 'n/a'. The section-7 enforcement cell writes three entity='gate' rows (dq_gate_blocking_evaluated, dq_gate_blocking_failures, dq_gate_raised) BEFORE the raise — task-026's persist-then-raise design preserved, not altered. silver-to-gold2's writer stamps producer='silver-to-gold2', status='n/a'. The double-counted run count is now filtered to its own producer, prints unattributed pre-change timestamps separately, and adds a per-producer breakdown.

CRITERION 5 SATISFIED BY ORCHESTRATOR (subagents cannot write .claude/): the breach_flag-vs-status passage was placed in data_quality_framework.md § 6 Alert Strategy, deliberately directly beneath the severity table that states the trap (score 0-49 => 'Block pipeline', which is NOT how the gate works). data_quality_architecture.md's 7-column schema tree and CREATE TABLE block were synced to 9 columns.

SHAPE DECISION filed as DEC-003 (entity='gate' rows vs a new gold_dq_gate_log table vs hoisting BLOCKING_CHECKS). Filed status=proposed for Erik's ratification, NOT self-approved — following rules/decisions.md over work-procedures.md § State Persistence Protocol, which conflict (FR-010/FR-011). Same correction verify-agent applied to DEC-002.
FRICTION: FR-012 (design_contradiction, framework doc severity table) and FR-013 (spec_implementation_gap, spec never mentions the gate) opened in the register.
CRITERION 6 UNMET BY DESIGN — [Erik/Fabric] deploy + one clean run + one deliberately-failed run (duplicate procurement transactions). Carries user_review_pending once verified.
VERIFIED 2026-07-23 attempt 1 — pass. runtime_validation not_applicable (Fabric-only PySpark; ast.parse + source reading is the local ceiling). Five minor residuals, none contract failures.

THIRD WRITER FOUND by verify-agent, not in the task's two-writer premise: sample-quality-data.Notebook appends a 7-column DataFrame to gold_quality_history, so its rows land NULL status/producer and read as unattributed pre-task-040 timestamps. Low risk (run-once task-017 seeder, guarded by an existing-sample early exit, not a pipeline activity) — but if it is ever re-run post-deploy, expect it in the producer IS NULL bucket.

files_affected corrected 2026-07-23 to include the two .claude/support/documents/ paths the orchestrator wrote for criterion 5 — declared scope was stale for future parallel-dispatch conflict detection.

task_verification.notes: Verified statically against git diff vs c510a3e; both notebooks are Fabric-only PySpark so nothing was executed — ast.parse (both clean) plus source reading is the local ceiling. Crit 1: explicit idempotent ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMNS in ensure_quality_history_columns() (silver-to-gold2:2218, mirrored data_quality_checks:1662); early-returns when table absent, guards each column against spark.table(...).columns; no mergeSchema/overwriteSchema/recreate/backfill near gold_quality_history. Crit 2 column-order: ADD COLUMNS appends at end; all three writers emit the same 9-column order matching CREATE TABLE and post-ALTER order — no silent column shear. Crit 2 durability: three entity='gate' rows appended at data_quality_checks:1936 BEFORE the raise at :1942-1950 — task-026's persist-then-raise preserved. Null-safety independently confirmed: all_check_results appended only inside log_check_result (:165, sole site), all 34 call sites pass a string literal or local `status`, all 12 `status =` assignments are ternaries over string literals; the one NULL (threshold on dq_gate_blocking_evaluated) is written under explicit StructType, not inference. Crit 4: run count filters producer='silver-to-gold2'; pre-change rows surfaced via a producer IS NULL count plus per-producer breakdown, reported not dropped. Crit 3 satisfied by producer + status='n/a'. Crit 5 verified in place, counter-example arithmetically correct (2 dup grains / 2561 => 99.92 vs 70.0 threshold, still status='fail'). Scope boundary held — BLOCKING_CHECKS, is_blocking, blocking counters and the single aggregated raise untouched. Cleared a latent interaction: the trend check reading gold_quality_history is scoped to metric_name='dq_overall_score' (:1418), so gate rows cannot perturb it. Crit 6 [Erik/Fabric] unmet by design — does not fail verification.

issues.description: Semantic model still carries the same double-count: OEMInsightBI_v2.SemanticModel/.../gold_quality_history.tmdl defines `Pipeline Runs Count = DISTINCTCOUNT(gold_quality_history[refresh_timestamp])` over the whole table, so Power BI keeps reporting ~2x the real run count. Out of contract (TMDL not in files_affected) but the same defect on the more user-visible surface.

THIRD writer to gold_quality_history not accounted for by the two-writer premise: fabric/sample-quality-data.Notebook still appends a 7-column DataFrame, so its rows would land with NULL status/producer and be counted as unattributed pre-task-040 timestamps. Low risk — run-once task-017 seeder, guarded by an existing-sample check that exits early, absent from the orchestrator pipeline.

data_quality_architecture.md now internally inconsistent: gold_quality_history section still reads Status | PLANNED (line 256, legend at 108/118) above the freshly-updated 9-column schema. Already routed — task-033 carries a note added 2026-07-23.

files_affected under-declared: criterion 5's deliverable landed in .claude/support/documents/data_quality_framework.md and data_quality_architecture.md (orchestrator-written) but neither path was listed.

Both data_quality_checks:1903's comment and data_quality_framework.md say 'status is pass or fail' under the entity='gate' query, but that query returns three rows and dq_gate_blocking_evaluated carries 'n/a'. The doc self-corrects two paragraphs later; the one-liner is imprecise rather than wrong.

test_protocol.summary: Confirm the gate verdict survives the run that produced it.

steps.instruction: After deploy, run the pipeline clean, then query gold_quality_history for the latest refresh_timestamp
steps.expected: The gate verdict (blocking-failure count / raised flag) is present and reads as passed, without consulting notebook source

steps.instruction: Inject a blocking failure (duplicate procurement transactions with an identical business signature) and re-run
steps.expected: Pipeline halts at the DQ activity AND the persisted verdict for that run records the blocking failure

steps.instruction: Query DISTINCT producers for a single run, and check silver-to-gold2's printed run count
steps.expected: Two producers distinguishable for one run; printed run count matches the real number of pipeline runs, not double

steps.instruction: On the first post-deploy run, read silver-to-gold2's output for the ALTER TABLE line and confirm a pre-change row still reads
steps.expected: 'added column status STRING' / 'producer STRING' printed once (and NOT on the second run — the helper is idempotent); SELECT on any pre-change refresh_timestamp returns its original 7 values with NULL status and NULL producer
